scope: Elastos minerinfo identity registry for RPC-decoded child miner identity labels.

source.name: project-maintained Elastos minerinfo registry
source.notes: Maps an Elastos minerinfo self-declared identity label to a project-stable pool slug ONLY when the label is itself a documented Bitcoin coinbase signature for the pool that the same-event BTC parent block independently reconciled to. Elastos minerinfo is RPC-decoded and NOT authenticated by the AuxPoW blob (elastos_rpc_decoded_unverified_by_auxpow); each mapping rests on documented-signature-string plus reconciled-BTC-parent agreement. Conflicted labels (e.g. 'Antpool', 'binance') and labels with no documented signature are intentionally omitted; see docs/attribution.md. Elastos reward addresses are mapped in the separate elastos_reward_address namespace (data/pools/child-identities/elastos_reward_address_registry.json), the more reliable Elastos identity key since minerinfo is spoofable.

evidence.btc_parent_pool_reconciliation: 98 of 98 active unresolved rows' same-event BTC parents reconciled to pool slug f2pool
evidence.note: The fish emoji is F2Pool's documented Bitcoin coinbase signature; the pool's operator handle is 七彩神仙鱼 ('Discus Fish'). The minerinfo label is itself the F2Pool signature, independent of the same-event BTC parent tag.

evidence.btc_parent_pool_reconciliation: 75 of 75 active unresolved rows' same-event BTC parents reconciled to pool slug viabtc
evidence.note: 'Mined by ViaBTC' is ViaBTC's documented Bitcoin coinbase signature. The minerinfo label is itself the ViaBTC signature, independent of the same-event BTC parent tag.
